data.title: Warren Buffett's Berkshire raises $1.9B in yen-denominated bonds - report
data.description:
Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE:BRK.A) (NYSE:BRK.B) raised $1.9 billion through a seven-tranche yen-denominated bond deal, according to a report by Reuters that cites a term sheet.

This issuance marks the largest of its kind for the company since its initial foray into Samurai bonds in 2019.

The U.S. investment giant issued bonds with varying maturities of 3, 5, 7, 10, 20, 28, and 30 years. The largest tranche was the 3-year bond, raising about $1.04 billion, followed by the 5-year bond that garnered ~$0.39 billion.

During the transaction, longer-dated bonds were introduced while a proposed 15-year tranche was ultimately dropped, the deal's bookrunners showed, as per the report.

The final pricing for each tranche was set at the lower to middle end of the revised price guidance provided to investors.

Berkshire (BRK.A) indicated that the proceeds will be used for general corporate purposes, although it did not disclose the size of the deal in the filing.

data.title: Redwood Trust prices reopening of $40M senior convertible notes
data.description:
• Redwood Trust (NYSE:RWT) priced ~$40M aggregate principal amount of its 7.75% convertible senior notes due 2027 in a private offering.
  • The notes will be issued as a reopening of, and will be part of the same series as, the 7.75% convertible senior notes due 2027 that the company originally issued in June 2022, of which $207.4M aggregate principal amount is currently outstanding.
  • The offering is expected to close on October 11, 2024.
  • Redwood (RWT) estimates that the net proceeds it will receive from the offering will be ~$38.2M.
  • Net proceeds will be used to repurchase a portion of the currently outstanding 5.75% exchangeable senior notes due 2025 previously issued by one of its subsidiaries.
  • The stock price traded marginally higher, up 1.2% on Wednesday during after hours of trade.

data.title: Dana gets new technology chief
data.description:
• Dana (NYSE:DAN) named Seth Metzger as senior VP and CTO, succeeding Christophe Dominiak who is retiring from Dana.
  • Mr. Metzger has been with Dana for more than two decades and has extensive experience in engineering and technology development.
  • He most recently served as senior VP of Electrification, as well as President of the Dana TM4 joint venture.

data.title: Asian markets mostly higher after Wall Street's overnight rally
data.description:
Asia-Pacific markets traded mostly higher on Thursday, with only China stocks trading marginally lower, after People’s Bank of China ((PBoC)) launched a CNY 500 billion swap facility.

The Japan (NKY:IND) +0.25% extending gains from the previous session and tracking a rally on Wall Street overnight.

The value of loans in Japan increased 2.7% Y/Y in September. Producer prices in Japan increased by 2.8% yoy in September 2024.

China (SHCOMP) -0.04% after the stocks retreaded sharply on the previous day, breaking a ten-day winning streak as an economic briefing earlier this week when authorities were expected to expound on policy plans disappointed markets by providing little details on further stimulus.

The CNY 500 billion swap facility will allow eligible financial institutions to use assets as collateral to secure liquidity.

Hong Kong (HSI) +4.26% after plummeting in the prior two sessions, boosted by record-high closes on the U.S. exchanges.

The South Korean won continued its recent decline toward 1,350 per dollar.

The trade deficit in the Philippines widened to $4.4 billion in August.

India (SENSEX) +0.57%.

Australia (AS51) rose +0.27% The seasonally adjusted private house approvals in Australia increased by 0.5% from the previous month to a 22-month high of 9,338 units in August. The seasonally adjusted estimate for total dwellings approved in Australia plunged 6.1% M/M. Consumer inflation expectations in Australia fell to 4.0% in October.

In the U.S., on Wednesday, all three major indexes ended higher with 9 out of the 11 S&P sectors finished higher, led by healthcare, technology and industrials. Investors also reacted to the Fed’s September meeting minutes, which revealed a "substantial majority" of officials supported a significant 50-basis-point rate cut, but left the outlook for future cuts uncertain.

U.S. stock futures were mixed on Thursday after the Dow and S&P 500 hit record closing highs in the previous session.

Dow +0.05%; S&P 500 -0.01%; Nasdaq +0.04%.

Investors geared up for September’s consumer inflation report.

Currencies: (JPY:USD), (CNY:USD), (AUD:USD), (INR:USD), (HKD:USD), (NZD:USD).

data.title: Avient raises dividend by 5% to $0.27
data.description:
• Avient (NYSE:AVNT) declares $0.27/share quarterly dividend, 4.9% increase from prior dividend of $0.26.

data.title: Thursday's economic calendar
data.description:
8:30 Jobless Claims

Initial Jobless Claims measures the number of individuals who filed for unemployment insurance for the first time during the past week. The number of people claiming unemployment benefits is expected to fall by 2000 in the last week after rising 6,000 in the prior week.

8:30 Consumer Price Index

The Consumer Price Index measures the change in the price of goods and services from the perspective of the consumer. It is a key way to measure changes in purchasing trends and inflation. CPI is expected to drop for the sixth time to 2.3% in September.

9:15 Fed's Cook: "Entrepreneurship and Innovation"

Federal Reserve Board Governor Lisa Cook speaks on "Entrepreneurship and Innovation" before the Women for Women Summit presented by the College of Charleston School of Business.

10:30 EIA Natural Gas Report

The Energy Information Administration Natural Gas Storage report measures the change in the number of cubic feet of natural gas held in underground storage over the prior week. US utilities added 55 billion cubic feet of gas into storage during the week ended September 27, 2024, below market expectations of a 59 bcf rise.

10:30 Fed's Barkin: "2025 Economic Outlook and Beyond"

Richmond Federal Reserve Bank President Thomas Barkin participates in fireside chat, "2025 Economic Outlook and Beyond" before the Virginia Maritime Association 2024 International Trade Symposium.

11:00 Fed's Williams: Economic Outlook and Monetary Policy

New York Federal Reserve Bank President John Williams gives remarks and participates in a moderated discussion on the economic outlook and monetary policy before event organized by Binghamton University.

1:00 PM 30-Year Note Auction

The auction indicates the percentage yield on 30-year bond auction. The US Treasury Department will hold an auction for $22 billion of 30-year auction. US 30 Year Bond Yield was 4.32 percent on Wednesday October 9, according to over-the-counter interbank yield quotes for this government bond maturity.

2:00 PM Treasury Statement

The Monthly Treasury Statement summarizes the financial activities of the federal government and off-budget federal entities and conforms to the Budget of the U.S. Government. The MTS presents a summary of: Receipts and outlays. Surplus or deficit.

4:30 PM Fed Balance Sheet

The Fed balance sheet is a weekly report listing the assets and liabilities of the Federal Reserve System.

Click here to see the economic calendar for the week

data.title: Oil futures slip as higher U.S. crude supplies outweigh Middle East, hurricane risks
data.description:
Crude oil futures added to the previous session's losses on Wednesday but settled well above the day's lows, after the U.S. government's weekly inventory report showed a larger than expected increase as production rose to 13.4M bbl/day, matching the record reached in August.

Commercial crude oil stocks, excluding the Strategic Petroleum Reserve, rose by 5.8M barrels to 422.7M barrels in the week ended Oct. 4, coming in ~4% below the five-year average for the time of year, the U.S. Energy Information Administration reported.

However, gasoline inventories fell by a larger than forecast 6.3M barrels to 214.9M barrels, which were ~4% below the five-year average, the EIA said.

Larger than expected drawdowns in gasoline and distillates, as well as a smaller than estimated build compared to estimates from the American Petroleum Institute, limited the day's decline in oil prices, Mizuho's Robert Yawger said.

Wednesday's selling extended the previous session's sharp drop on reports of talks over a potential Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire, which returned some of the big gains made last week on concerns that Israel could strike Iranian oil infrastructure in retaliation for a missile attack.

Weak demand continues to underpin the fundamental outlook, with the EIA on Tuesday lowering its world oil demand forecast for 2025 to 104.3M bbl/day, growing 1.2M bbl/day from 2024 but ~300K bbl/day below prior forecasts, citing weakening economic activity in China and North America.

Front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for November delivery ended -0.4% to $73.24/bbl after falling as low as $71.53, while front-month December Brent crude (CO1:COM) closed -0.8% to $76.58/bbl, the lowest settlement value since October 2 for both benchmarks.

Also, front-month November Nymex natural gas (NG1:COM) finished -2.6% to $2.660/MMBtu, a fourth straight daily loss, with analysts pointing to anticipated demand destruction from Hurricane Milton, weaker ERCOT demand, and the potential for eventually stronger U.S. production.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

Nearly 25% of gasoline stations in Florida were out of fuel Wednesday afternoon, according to GasBuddy, as Gulf Coast residents rushed to evacuate ahead of Hurricane Milton, which could become one of the most destructive storms ever to hit the area.

Analysts say Milton's severity and predicted path near the Tampa Bay area, where most of Florida's fuel import and storage terminals are located, make it potentially the most devastating hit to the state's fuel markets in more than 30 years.

The hurricane likely will not have a meaningful impact on production in the Gulf of Mexico, although demand likely has been front-loaded, with people evacuating to seek shelter before Milton's landfall.

The "net impact of Milton is likely going to be reduced consumer demand as residents shelter in place and are not likely to be commuting or going to school for the rest of the week, or potentially longer," Sevens Report Research co-editor Tyler Richey said.

data.title: U.S. weekly rail traffic falls 2.5% year-over-year
data.description:
• The association of American Railroads [AAR] reported on Wednesday that for the week ending October 5, 2024, total U.S. weekly rail traffic was 486,187 carloads and intermodal units, down 2.5% year-over-year.
  • Total carloads for the week fell 3.5% year-over-year to 225,280 carloads, while U.S. weekly intermodal volume was 260,907 containers and trailers, falling 1.7%.
  • Four of the 10 carload commodity groups posted a year-over-year increase for the week. They included farm products, excluding grain and food, chemicals, metallic ores, and metals.
  • Commodity groups that fell year-over-year for the week included coal, nonmetallic minerals, and grain.
  • North American rail volume for the week, on 9 reporting U.S., Canadian and Mexican railroads, was 331,587 carloads, down 4.5% and 341,107 intermodal units, down 3%.
  • Canadian railroads reported 94,571 carloads for the week, down 2%, and 68,805 intermodal units, down 4.7%.
  • Mexican railroads reported 11,736 carloads for the week, down 31.7% compared with the same week last year, and 11,395 intermodal units, down 19.3%.
  • Related Tickers: Canadian Pacific Railway (CP), Canadian National Railway (CNI), CSX Corp. (CSX), Union Pacific (UNP), Norfolk Southern (NSC), and Brookfield Infrastructure Partners (BIP) (BIPC).

data.title: OpenAI projections suggest no profits until 2029: report
data.description:
• Microsoft-backed (NASDAQ:MSFT) OpenAI is not expected to earn annual profits until 2029, according to company financial documents obtained by The Information.
  • The documents show annual losses could strike as high as $14B in 2026, the report said.
  • The projections showed the company reaching annual revenue of $100B in 2029. OpenAI also expects to spend about $200B through the end of the decade, minus stock compensations. Losses between 2023 and 2028 are projected to total $44B.
  • The report also found that Microsoft is entitled to 20% of OpenAI's revenue.
  • Last week, OpenAI completed its latest funding round totaling $6.6B, which propelled the company to a $157B valuation. Microsoft and Nvidia (NVDA) both participated in the round.

data.title: Canaccord Genuity Group appoints Nadine Ahn as Deputy Chief Financial Officer
data.description:
Canaccord Genuity Group (TSX: CF) on Wednesday announced the appointment of Nadine Ahn as Deputy Chief Financial Officer, Canaccord Genuity Group (TSX:CF:CA).

The company's current Chief Financial Officer (("CFO")), Don MacFayden has informed the Company of his desire to transition from his role as the Company's CFO in the coming year. This appointment anticipates Ahn transitioning to the CFO role in calendar 2025 as part of the company's succession plan.

After Ahn assumes the CFO role, MacFayden will continue to be active within the Company's U.S. capital markets business, Canaccord Genuity, where he has been in a senior financial leadership position for a number of years.

Most recently Ahn served as Chief Financial Officer of a major full-service Canadian bank from 2021 to 2024.

Source: Press Release
